pr.title: ftFox - all functions complete and matching
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Completed and matched the remaining ftFox assembly by decompiling Fox/Falco side special and down special code into C, wiring the new objects into the build, and replacing anonymous action-state table targets with named ftFox_SpecialS and ftFox_SpecialLw callbacks. The PR also expanded ftfox.h and fighter.h with action-state IDs, coll flag macros, prototypes, and Fighter state-var union aliases needed by Illusion/Phantasm and Reflector logic. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from changed code only.
what_changed: Removed roughly 3 KB of ftFox assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftfox.s and replaced it with matching C implementations split across ftFox_SpecialLw.c and the new ftFox_SpecialS.c.
Added ftFox_SpecialLw.c implementations for Reflector start, loop, hit, turn, end, ground-to-air, air-to-ground, physics, collision, IASA, GFX, and reflect-hit creation behavior.

Added ftFox_SpecialS.c implementations for Fox Illusion / Falco Phantasm start, dash, end, ground/air transitions, ghost item/GFX handling, and helper accessors such as ftFox_SpecialS_CheckGhostRemove, ftFox_SpecialS_GetCmdVar2, ftFox_SpecialS_CopyStructVars, and ftFox_SpecialS_GetFloatVar.

Updated both Fox and Falco action-state tables in assembly to point to named symbols like ftFox_SpecialSStart_Anim, ftFox_SpecialAirS_Coll, ftFox_SpecialLwLoop_Phys, and ftFox_SpecialAirLwTurn_Coll instead of raw func_800E.... names.
Updated asm/melee/it/code_8027CF30.s branch targets to call the newly named ftFox_SpecialS helper functions used by the Illusion/Phantasm ghost item logic.

Expanded ftfox.h with SpecialLw action-state enum entries, FTFOX_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG, FTFOX_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG, renamed Illusion attribute fields, and prototypes for the new SpecialLw and SpecialS functions.
Updated include/functions.h by replacing unknown func_800E.... declarations with named ftFox_SpecialLw and ftFox_SpecialS declarations, still as unresolved comment-style prototypes.

Extended Fighter unions in fighter.h with aliases needed by this decomp, including x2344_foxSpecialS, x2374_foxArray, x2384_GObj, signed variants for x2384/x2388, an ftFox_SpecialS_Struct typedef, and a getFtAttrs inline helper.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftFox/ftFox_SpecialS.c.o to obj_files.mk so the new C translation unit participates in the text object build.
smart_moves: Kept Fox and Falco shared move behavior under ftFox_* names and updated Falco's action-state table to use those symbols, matching the apparent shared implementation of Fox Illusion / Falco Phantasm and Reflector callbacks.

Introduced semantic Fighter union aliases for move-local scratch storage instead of leaving all accesses as raw offsets; examples include x2208_ftcmd_var2, x2340_stateVar1, x2348_stateVar3, x234C_stateVar4_s32, x2344_foxSpecialS, x2374_foxArray, and x2384_GObj.
Captured action-state transition flag bundles as FTFOX_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG and FTFOX_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG macros, making repeated Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls easier to audit and reuse.

Preserved important behavior through small helper functions: SpecialS ghost removal checking by action-state range, ftcmd var access, copying Vec3 state arrays, and reading float arrays used by item/ghost code.

Used inline helper routines in the C decomp for repeated setup/physics patterns, such as Reflector variable setup and shared air Reflector physics, reducing duplication while still matching the original code.
Embedded decomp.me scratch links in comments for many functions, providing traceability for future matching work even though the PR had no explanatory body.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter move is shared between clone characters, verify and update every action-state table that references the code; this PR renamed Falco table entries to the ftFox_* symbols rather than treating Falco as separate code.

For fighter special moves, naming the full callback matrix with the pattern Start/Loop/Hit/End/Turn plus Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll makes action-state tables searchable and reduces ambiguity compared with address-based func_ names.

Move-local Fighter scratch offsets should be given contextual union aliases as soon as their usage is understood; this enables readable C and helps related item code call back into fighter state safely.
Item assembly may depend on fighter helper symbols. After decompiling fighter functions, update item-side branch labels to the new names so cross-system dependencies remain explicit.
Action-state transition flags are worth macroizing when reused across ground/air conversions and state restarts; this avoids scattered magic flag expressions and makes matching intent clearer.
Header work is part of matching work: adding enum values, prototypes, coll flags, attribute field names, and struct aliases was necessary to move ftFox code out of asm cleanly.
naming_conventions: Fighter special move functions use ftFox_Special<Move><State>_<Callback>, for example ftFox_SpecialLwStart_Anim, ftFox_SpecialLwLoop_Coll, ftFox_SpecialSStart_Phys, and ftFox_SpecialSEnd_Action.
Aerial variants insert Air after Special, for example ftFox_SpecialAirLwStart_Anim and ftFox_SpecialAirS_AirToGround.
Ground/air transition helpers use GroundToAir and AirToGround suffixes.
Move-start entry points use StartAction suffix, such as ftFox_SpecialLw_StartAction and ftFox_SpecialAirS_StartAction.

Helper functions describe behavioral purpose rather than address, such as ftFox_SpecialS_CheckGhostRemove, ftFox_SpecialS_GetCmdVar2, ftFox_SpecialS_CopyStructVars, ftFox_SpecialLw_CreateReflectHit, and ftFox_SpecialLw_CreateReflectGFX.
Fox attribute fields were made more semantic where evidence existed, e.g. x34_FOX_ILLUSION_GROUND_END_VEL_X, x3C_FOX_ILLUSION_AIR_END_VEL_X, and x4C_FOX_ILLUSION_FREEFALL_MOBILITY.
Collision flag macros follow FTFOX_SPECIAL*_COLL_FLAG naming, including FTFOX_SPECIALLW_COLL_FLAG and FTFOX_SPECIALS_COLL_FLAG.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced large ftfox.s text with C while leaving action-state table data in assembly and retargeting entries to named C symbols.
Used existing action-state callback ordering in as_table_falco/as_table_fox to validate the mapping from raw func_800E addresses to Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll names.
Preserved exact state transitions through Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC parameters, including current animation frame reuse and flag combinations for ground/air conversions.
Matched array-like scratch memory by adding zero-length or contextual aliases inside Fighter unions, such as Vec3 x2344_foxSpecialS[0] and f32 x2374_foxArray[0].
Used callback fields like x21BC_callback_Accessory4, x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, and x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag to recreate GFX/accessory behavior rather than leaving it as opaque offset stores.

Kept some questionable matching idioms visible in the diff, such as repeated assignments like fp = getFighter(fighter_gobj) and fp = fp = fighter_gobj->user_data, which likely existed to satisfy compiler output.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer guidance or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on title, changed files, and diff evidence only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or clean up ftFox_SpecialLw state variables x2340/x2348/x234C or remove matching-only duplicate assignments.
Search for subsequent reviews touching ftFox_SpecialS.c or ftFox_SpecialLw.c to see whether any prototypes were moved out of include/functions.h.
Look for later changes to ftFalco action-state tables to confirm whether shared ftFox_* naming remained the convention for Falco Phantasm/Reflector code.
Search item code around code_8027CF30.s for eventual C decomp that consumes ftFox_SpecialS_GetCmdVar2, ftFox_SpecialS_CopyStructVars, and ftFox_SpecialS_GetFloatVar.
